While it would not be accurate to say that the GMAT never uses evidence from one situation to support an argument about another situation, it is accurate to say that comparisons like those in the examples above will rarely constitute the basis for a Strengthen correct answer.
Here’s an example of how these not well-connected choices work in context:
In response to residents’ complaints about constant noise, the city council of Mergate has decided to permanently close large public Mergate Airport. Even though many businesses in Mergate depend on spending by vacationers, the members of the city council believe that closing the airport will result in increased rather than decreased profitability for such businesses in Mergate.
Which of the following, if true, provides the strongest support for the city council members’ prediction?
(Not Well Connected Choice) When a small private airport was shut down in Boselton, some businesses in Boselton experienced increases in revenue.
(Correct Answer) Without the constant noise made by airplanes taking off and landing at Mergate Airport, Mergate will be a much more attractive destination for vacationers.
To get this one correct, we would have to find the choice that supports the following conclusion:
Conclusion: Closing Mergate Airport will result in increased rather than decreased profitability for many businesses in Mergate that depend on spending by vacationers.
Does the not well-connected choice provide solid support for this conclusion? Not really.
The correct answer, on the other hand, provides strong support for the conclusion that closing Mergate Airport will result in increased profitability for businesses in Mergate that depend on spending by vacationers, since it provides a reason to believe that, as a result of the closing of the airport, Mergate will become a much more attractive destination, and more vacationers will go there and spend money.
💡MUST KNOW:
When answering a Strengthen question, look for a choice that provides information that is clearly connected to the scenario presented in the argument.
Consider the following example:
--- primary_color: green secondary_color: lightgray text_color: black shuffle_questions: false shuffle_answers: false --- ## Example 17 CSim Labs creates simulators that can be used to train people to use heavy construction equipment before they actually operate the equipment. Which of the following, if true, provides the most additional support to the conclusion above? 1. [ ] (A) The use of simulators that pilots can use to simulate the operation of aircraft has contributed significantly to the high level of safety currently existing in civil aviation. > Correct Answer: (B) 2. [x] (B) A construction equipment operator in training using a simulator can learn to safely handle types of challenging situations that an operator might otherwise not be prepared to handle. 3. [ ] (C) When operators train by using actual construction equipment, the companies training the operators can incur significant expenses for fuel and equipment maintenance. > Correct Answer: (B) 4. [ ] (D) Classroom training for young drivers has been shown to reduce the probability of drivers’ getting into crashes during their first few years of driving. > Correct Answer: (B) 5. [ ] (E) Simulators for training operators of construction equipment can be tailored for training operators in the use of a wide variety of types of construction equipment. > Correct Answer: (B) View Solution Question Level: easy The passage presents the following conclusion:
Conclusion: Companies that use CSim Lab’s simulators to train operators to operate heavy construction equipment will avoid many mishaps.
The passage supports that conclusion via the following premises:
Premise: Simulators can be used to train people to use heavy construction equipment before they actually operate the equipment.
Premise: Many operating mishaps occur when operators are first learning how to operate heavy construction equipment.
So, the reasoning is that, if operators can learn to use the equipment before they actually operate it, mishaps that would have occurred as the operators were learning will be avoided.
The correct answer will provide additional support for the same conclusion. In considering the choices, we must take into account how well connected the information presented in each choice is to the conclusion of the argument.
(A) The use of simulators that pilots can use to simulate the operation of aircraft has contributed significantly to the high level of safety currently existing in civil aviation. While the use of simulators in training pilots seems similar to using simulators in training construction equipment operators, the GMAT would not consider the fact that use of simulators in aviation contributed to a high level of safety strong evidence that, by using simulators in a different venue, construction, companies would avoid mishaps that would otherwise occur when operators are first learning how to use heavy construction equipment. This choice is not well enough connected to the scenario presented in the passage to be considered support for the conclusion.
CORRECT ANSWER(B) A construction equipment operator in training using a simulator can learn to safely handle types of challenging situations that an operator might otherwise not be prepared to handle. If, through using a simulator, a construction equipment operator can learn to safely handle challenging situations that an operator might not otherwise be prepared to handle, then that operator might avoid experiencing mishaps in those types of situations. So, this choice adds support for the conclusion.
YOUR CHOICE(C) When operators train by using actual construction equipment, the companies training the operators can incur significant expenses for fuel and equipment maintenance. This choice supports the wrong conclusion, the conclusion that companies can reduce expenses by using simulators, rather than the conclusion that companies can avoid mishaps by using simulators.
(D) Classroom training for young drivers has been shown to reduce the probability of drivers’ getting into crashes during their first few years of driving. This choice discusses a situation in which training helps with avoiding mishaps. However, the use of classroom training, rather than simulators, in the training of young drivers, rather than equipment operators, is not sufficiently connected to the scenario presented in the passage to be considered strong support for the conclusion.
(E) Simulators for training operators of construction equipment can be tailored for training operators in the use of a wide variety of types of construction equipment. This choice supports the wrong conclusion. We need something that supports the conclusion that training via the use of simulators will have certain effects. This choice supports the conclusion that simulators can be widely used.
Correct Answer: (B) Let’s now talk about another type of incorrect choice, choices that are connected to the scenarios presented and seem to support the conclusions but don’t actually get the job done.
summary:
Lesson Content A typical argument in a Strengthen question is based on a scenario that involves, for example, a particular place, company, phenomenon, or point in time.
💡MUST KNOW:
In answering a Strengthen question, we must take care not to select as correct choices that aren’t well-connected to the scenario in the argument.
So, what does it mean to be “not well-connected?” The truth of the matter is that what the GMAT considers not well-connected might be different from what we’d expect (sorry – don’t kill the messenger).
title: 4.17 Strengthen Incorrect Choice Type 5: Information not Well Connected to the Scenario in the Argument

contents:
Lesson Content Some answers to Strengthen questions are subtly written in a way such that they seem to strengthen the arguments, and, at the same time, they don’t really get the job done.
💡MUST KNOW:
Some answers to Strengthen questions are subtly written in a way such that they seem to strengthen the arguments, and, at the same time, they don’t really get the job done. So, we could call them “pseudo-strengtheners.”
A pseudo-strengthener may say something that does not conflict with the argument’s conclusion but does not support the conclusion either.
In this example, the pseudo-strengthener choice neither conflicts with nor supports the conclusion:
A new method of changing the color of a car involves wrapping the car in colored vinyl rather than repainting the car in a new color. The makers of the colored vinyl expect most car owners who want to change the colors of their cars to unconventional colors to select this method over repainting their cars.
Which of the following, if true, provides the most support for the expectation?
(Pseudo-Strengthener Choice) The vinyl used for changing the colors of cars to unconventional colors is produced via the use of innovative manufacturing techniques that did not exist until recently.
(Correct Answer) Since, unlike paint, vinyl is easy to remove, someone selling a vinyl wrapped car can easily restore the car to its original color, which is likely to be a color popular with potential buyers.
Here’s the expectation for which we have to find support:
Conclusion: Most car owners who want to change the colors of their cars to unconventional colors will select vinyl wrapping over repainting.
The pseudo-strengthener answer choice is a tempting trap, since it uses the wording “innovative manufacturing techniques that did not exist until recently” to make the vinyl sound new and exciting and, thus, could be perceived as indicating that people will choose vinyl wrapping.
Notice, however, the issue with the pseudo-strengthener choice.
In other words, OK, great.
So, the pseudo-strengthener choice provides absolutely no support for the conclusion.
The correct answer, on the other hand, provides clear support for the expectation that most people who want to change the colors of their cars to unconventional colors will choose vinyl wrapping over repainting.
Now, let’s consider another type of example.
Transportation company GreenPS uses electric trucks to transport shipments via highway. Even though its trucks already travel at the maximum allowable speed on the highways, the logistics experts at GreenPS maintain that, as GreenPS replaces the trucks that it is currently using with new models of electric trucks, it will be able to move shipments from place to place faster.
Which of the following, if true, supports the logistics experts’ contention?
(Pseudo Strengthener Choice) Since the batteries that power the new trucks are more powerful than the batteries in the currently used trucks, the new trucks are capable of achieving higher speeds.
(Correct Answer) Unlike the currently used trucks, the new trucks are equipped with technology such that their batteries can be fully charged in minutes, rather than in an hour, at highway stops.
Here’s the conclusion that we have to support:
Conclusion: As GreenPS replaces the trucks that it is currently using with new models of electric trucks, it will be able to move shipments from place to place faster.
The pseudo strengthener choice is rather tempting, as the passage says that GreenPS transports shipments by highway, and so it seems to make sense that a new truck that can achieve a speed higher than can be achieved by currently used trucks could transports shipments faster than they are transported currently.
Notice, however, that the passage has ruled out this potential strengthener, by saying that GreenPS’s trucks already travel at the maximum allowable speed on the highways.
The correct answer, on the other hand, supports the contention, by providing reason to believe that the new trucks will get from place to place faster.
💡MUST KNOW:
When answering a Strengthen question, beware of answer choices that seem to support the conclusion but don’t really get the job done.
Consider the following examples:
--- primary_color: green secondary_color: lightgray text_color: black shuffle_questions: false shuffle_answers: false --- ## Example 18 Casinos utilize pastel colors, including light greens, purples, and blues, in the screens of their digital betting games because these colors are the most visually soothing colors. Which of the following, if true, does the most to strengthen the argument above? 1. [ ] (A) Non-pastel colors, such as bright reds, oranges, and yellows, are rarely used on the TV screens of commercial airplanes to catch the attention of passengers during safety demonstrations. > Correct Answer: (C) 2. [ ] (B) Game technicians employed by casinos know how to switch the screens of digital betting games from using pastel colors to using non-pastel colors. > Correct Answer: (C) 3. [x] (C) The more the colors used in the screens of digital casino betting games catch people’s attention, the more money casinos will earn on those games. 4. [ ] (D) Casinos often seek to gain the attention of potential customers by using gaudy décor involving bright colors. > Correct Answer: (C) 5. [ ] (E) Non-pastel colors, including bright reds, oranges, and yellows, are used in traffic lights because these colors rapidly catch the attention of drivers of vehicles. > Correct Answer: (C) View Solution Question Level: medium Breakdown of the argument:
Background: Casinos utilize pastel colors, including light greens, purples, and blues, in the screens of their digital betting games because these colors are the most visually soothing colors.
Background: When a gambler feels visually soothed, he or she is more likely to play casino games for an extended period of time.
Premise: Non-pastel colors, such as bright reds, oranges, and yellows, are proven to catch people’s attention much better than pastel colors.
Conclusion: Casinos that switch to using non-pastel colors in the screens of their digital betting games will maximize the money they earn on those games.
See the gap in the author’s reasoning?
He says that, because non-pastel colors, such as bright reds, oranges, and yellows, are proven to catch people’s attention much better than pastel colors, casinos that switch to using non-pastel colors in the screens of their digital betting games will maximize the money they earn on those games.
Notice that, at the moment, there is no clear link between “the catching of people’s attention in the games” and “the maximizing of the money they earn on those games.” The author must assume that the more the colors used in the screens of digital casino betting games catch people’s attention, the more money casinos will earn on those games.
Our job is to find an answer choice that strengthens the argument.
(A) Non-pastel colors, such as bright reds, oranges, and yellows, are rarely used on the TV screens of commercial airplanes to catch the attention of passengers during safety demonstrations. What this choice says goes in the wrong direction.
After all, safety demonstrations on airplanes would certainly seem to be something one would want people to pay attention to. So then, why are non-pastel colors rarely used on the TV screens of commercial airplanes to catch the attention of passengers during safety demonstrations?
To be clear, what this answer choice says does not weaken the argument, because we already know from the passage that non-pastel colors are proven to catch people’s attention much better than pastel colors, and catching the attention or airline passengers might be quite different from catching the attention of gamblers. However, it certainly does not go in the direction we’re seeking; we’re seeking a choice that strengths the argument, and this choice certainly does not.
It’s always helpful to be cognizant of the direction in which an answer choice takes us.
(B) Game technicians employed by casinos know how to switch the screens of digital betting games from using pastel colors to using non-pastel colors. This choice is tempting because it supports a conclusion related to the author’s conclusion. It supports the conclusion that switching to non-pastel colors would be relatively easy for casinos to do. After all, if game technicians employed by casinos know how to switch the screens of digital betting games from using pastel colors to using non-pastel colors, then it seems likely that making the switch would be no big deal.
Of course, the author’s conclusion is not about how easy it would be for casinos to make the switch; it’s that making the switch will result in the casinos’ maximizing the money they earn on digital betting games.
So, this choice provides support for the wrong conclusion and has no effect on the strength of the argument.
CORRECT ANSWER(C) The more the colors used in the screens of digital casino betting games catch people’s attention, the more money casinos will earn on those games. Notice that what this answer choice says strengthens the argument by stating as fact an assumption made by the author in making his argument.
As mentioned in the introduction to this explanation, the author assumes that the more the colors used in the screens of digital casino betting games catch people’s attention, the more money casinos will earn on those games.
Is that assumption a good one or a bad one? If it is a good assumption, the argument is stronger. If it is a bad one, the argument is weaker.
What this answer choice does is confirm that that assumption made by the author is a good one, by stating that assumption as fact.
Whenever we can take an assumption made by an author and turn that assumption into a premise (a fact), the argument will be stronger.
(D) Casinos often seek to gain the attention of potential customers by using gaudy décor involving bright colors. This choice is a tempting pseudo-strengthener.
The fact that casinos often seek to gain the attention of potential customers by using gaudy décor involving bright colors may seem to strengthen the support for the conclusion that casinos that switch to using non-pastel colors in the screens of their digital betting games will maximize the money they earn on those games. It could seem to indicate that using bright colors, rather than pastel colors, in the games is a good idea.
However, the truth is that what this choice says makes no difference in the support for the conclusion.
After all, the fact that casinos seek to gain the attention of potential customers by using gaudy décor involving bright colors does not indicate anything about whether casinos will maximize earnings by using bright colors rather than pastel colors in digital betting machines. The passage tells us that pastel colors are soothing and that bright colors are attention getting, and this choice does not tell us whether soothing gamblers using digital betting machines or getting their attention results in greater earnings.
In fact, this choice does not even tell us whether casinos that seek to gain the attention of potential customers by using gaudy décor involving bright colors succeed in any way as a result of their using bright colors.
All this choice tells us is that casinos often use bright colors to seek to gain attention, and we already know from the passage that bright colors attract attention.
In order to avoid choosing a pseudo-strengthener like this one, we have to pay attention to the effects of the answer choices we are considering rather than go with something that seems to in some vague way strengthen an argument.
YOUR CHOICE(E) Non-pastel colors, including bright reds, oranges, and yellows, are used in traffic lights because these colors rapidly catch the attention of drivers of vehicles. We must remember that the premises provided to us in the argument are statements of fact. There is no way to strengthen or “make better” a statement of fact.
What have we been told by the passage? We have been told that non-pastel colors, such as bright reds, oranges, and yellows, are proven to catch people’s attention much better than pastel colors. We can’t make that fact any more of a fact.
However, if you chose this choice as the correct answer, you tried to do just that. You said, “If non-pastel colors, including bright reds, oranges, and yellows, are used in traffic lights because these colors rapidly catch the attention of drivers of vehicles, then we have more faith that the author’s plan will work.”
However, we already know that non-pastel colors, such as bright reds, oranges, and yellows, are proven to catch people’s attention much better than pastel colors. So, although what this answer choice says certainly goes in the same direction as the argument, it does not strengthen it.
Correct Answer: (C) --- primary_color: green secondary_color: lightgray text_color: black shuffle_questions: false shuffle_answers: false --- ## Example 19 Last year, Brilliant Broadcasting doubled, from the amount that it spent the year before, the amount of money that it spent to advertise to potential viewers its hit television show Who wants to win the most? All the same, television rating services indicated that the average number of people who watched Who wants to win the most? each week last year was virtually unchanged from the average number who watched the show each week the year before. Which of the following, if true, most strongly supports the executives’ claim? 1. [x] (A) Last year, two other major broadcasting companies began airing shows that were essentially thinly disguised but well produced copies of Who wants to win the most? 2. [ ] (B) Brilliant Broadcasting makes past episodes of Who wants to win the most? available for online viewing. > Correct Answer: (A) 3. [ ] (C) Who wants to win the most? was Brilliant Broadcasting’s most popular show last year. > Correct Answer: (A) 4. [ ] (D) Last year, the percentage of people who knew what time of day Who wants to win the most? comes on was greater than the percentage who knew the previous year. > Correct Answer: (A) 5. [ ] (E) The numbers provided by the television rating services are not exact counts of viewers but rather estimates based on samples. > Correct Answer: (A) View Solution Question Level: hard To answer this question, we need a choice that supports the following claim made by Brilliant Broadcasting’s executives:
Conclusion: The increase in advertising spending paid off.
The executives’ claim seems to be in conflict with the information presented in the passage, as the company doubled spending on advertising, but the number of viewers of the show did not increase.
To get this question correct, we have to find a choice that supports the executives’ claim despite the fact that the number of viewers of the show did not increase.
YOUR CHOICE(A) Last year, two other major broadcasting companies began airing shows that were essentially thinly disguised but well produced copies of Who wants to win the most? If, last year, two other major broadcasters began airing shows that were essentially copies of Who wants to win the most?, one might have reasonably expected that those two new shows would have taken some viewers from Who wants to win the most?. However, even though there were two new shows that could have taken its viewers, Who wants to win the most? had the same number of viewers last year as it had the year before.
The fact that its number of viewers didn’t decrease, even though there were two shows that could have taken some of its viewers, could be taken as an indication that the advertising paid off, by keeping the number of viewers the same in the face of new competition.
So, this choice supports the claim that the increase in spending paid off.
(B) Brilliant Broadcasting makes past episodes of Who wants to win the most? available for online viewing. We could spin a story about how the extra advertising probably somehow paid off through increases in online viewers, even as the number of people who watched Who wants to win the most? on television remained the same.
However, this choice does not provide us with any information on the number of online viewers. It tells us only that the shows are available online. So, the idea that the advertising spending paid off by affecting the number of online viewers has no basis in information that we are given.
In answering Critical Reasoning questions, we have to be careful not to concoct unsupported convoluted stories.
(C) Who wants to win the most? was Brilliant Broadcasting’s most popular show last year. This choice is a pseudo-strengthener, as what it says about the relative popularity of the show could seem to indicate that the advertising spending paid off.
However, the truth is that this choice does not support the claim. For one thing, it could be that none of Brilliant Broadcasting’s other shows are popular at all, and so Who wants to win the most? is simply popular by default.
Whatever the case is, a comparison of the popularity of Who wants to win the most? with the popularity of the company’s other shows does not give us a clear reason to believe that the advertising spending paid off. After all, even if what this choice says is true, the fact remains that the number of viewers of Who wants to win the most? did not increase.
(D) Last year, the percentage of people who knew what time of day Who wants to win the most? comes on was greater than the percentage who knew the previous year. This choice is a pseudo-strengthener, as the fact that more people know what time the show is on could seem to indicate that the increase in advertising spending paid off.
However, the truth is that the passage has already stated that the number of viewers did not increase, and an increase in the number of people who know what time the show is on without any increase in viewers is virtually worthless to Brilliant Broadcasting. So, regardless of the fact that there was an increase in the number of people who knew what time the show is on, we still don’t have support for the conclusion that there was any actual payoff from the increase in advertising spending.
(E) The numbers provided by the television rating services are not exact counts of viewers but rather estimates based on samples. The fact that the numbers provided by the television rating services are not exact counts of viewers but rather estimates based on samples could be taken as indicating that it is possible that the agencies’ numbers are incorrect and that, therefore, it is possible that, in actuality, there was an increase in viewers of Who wants to win the most?.
However, having a reason to consider something possible is not the same as having support for the conclusion that it is true.
So, this choice provides no support for the claim that the increase in advertising spending did pay off.
Correct Answer: (A) Let’s wrap up our discussion of Strengthen question incorrect choice types by discussing choices that do the opposite of strengthen arguments.
summary:
Lesson Content Some answers to Strengthen questions are subtly written in a way such that they seem to strengthen the arguments, and, at the same time, they don’t really get the job done.
💡MUST KNOW:
Some answers to Strengthen questions are subtly written in a way such that they seem to strengthen the arguments, and, at the same time, they don’t really get the job done. So, we could call them “pseudo-strengtheners.
title: 4.18 Strengthen Incorrect Choice Type 6: Pseudo-Strengthener

contents:
Lesson Content If the goal in answering a Strengthen question is to find an answer choice that strengthens the argument, then clearly a choice that weakens the argument must be incorrect.
However, when a test-taker is under the pressure of the test, answers that weaken rather than strengthen the arguments in Strengthen questions can be tempting because they are closely related to the given information and, thus, can seem to provide just the type of information needed to answer the question.
It’s easier than you might think to lose track and choose a weakening answer choice when we need a strengthener, because an answer of either type can be relevant to the argument.
So, when answering a Strengthen question, be on the lookout for answer choices that actually weaken the author’s argument, and eliminate them, by remembering what type of answer you need and paying attention to the effects of answer choices you are considering.
💡MUST KNOW:
When you are answering a Strengthen question, be careful not to get tempted by relevance or wording into choosing an answer that weakens rather than strengthens the argument.
Let’s apply this awareness in the context of our Golado example:
The government of Golado has recently increased the taxes imposed on the sale of all alcoholic beverages in Golado. While the tax increase is sure to result in a significant decrease in consumption of alcohol in Golado, government officials believe that increasing the taxes on sales of alcohol will result in a reduction in Golado’s government budget gap.
Which of the following, if true, provides evidence suggesting that the government officials’ belief is correct?
(Incorrect Choice that Weakens) A reduction in alcohol consumption resulting from the tax increase is expected to result in a reduction in the fees the government of Golado collects from importers of alcohol.
(Correct Answer) In Golado, all of the organizations involved in dealing with health problems resulting from the consumption of alcohol are funded by the government.
To correctly answer the question, we need an answer that supports the conclusion that increasing the taxes on sales of alcohol will result in a reduction in Golado’s government budget gap.
However, under the pressure of the test, a test-taker might lose track and choose an answer like the incorrect choice that weakens, because what it says is clearly relevant to a discussion of the budget of Golado.
Of course, as we have discussed, the correct answer, which at first glance may seem less relevant to the discussion of the budget than the incorrect choice that weakens, does support the conclusion.
Consider the following example:
--- primary_color: green secondary_color: lightgray text_color: black shuffle_questions: false shuffle_answers: false --- ## Example 20 65 million years ago, a mass extinction of dinosaurs occurred. Which of the following, if true, does the most to confirm the validity of the explanation given? 1. [ ] (A) A group of volcanoes located in Asia were unusually active around the time of the dinosaur mass extinction, ejecting large amounts of matter into the atmosphere. > Correct Answer: (C) 2. [ ] (B) Fossil evidence indicates that, 65 million years ago, the number of species of dinosaurs inhabiting Earth rapidly decreased. > Correct Answer: (C) 3. [x] (C) The chemical composition of the layer of sediment deposited worldwide 65 million years ago resembles that of material found in the area where the asteroid landed in Yucatan. 4. [ ] (D) Near the Yucatan crater, material created by the asteroid impact was found underneath deposits of material that was created thousands of years before the dinosaur mass extinction. > Correct Answer: (C) 5. [ ] (E) A dust cloud could affect the environment by reflecting much of the sunlight that would otherwise reach the planet. > Correct Answer: (C) View Solution Question Level: hard To correctly answer this question, we need additional support for the following explanation:
Conclusion: It is likely that the dinosaur mass extinction was caused by the impact of an asteroid in the Yucatan area.
Notice that the author is making a cause-and-effect claim. Has the author presented sufficient evidence to support this cause and-effect-claim?
Not exactly. The passage has supported the explanation with evidence indicating that an asteroid impacted Earth and that a layer of sediment deposited at the time of the mass extinction came from a dust cloud that could have been created by an asteroid impact and that severely altered Earth’s environment.
Ok, great. But what’s missing? Something important. It’s not clear that the asteroid actually caused the dust cloud. Remember, the fact that one event precedes a second event does not allow us to conclude that the first event caused the second. The argument could be strengthened by information more clearly connecting the asteroid with the dust cloud.
(A) A group of volcanoes located in Asia were unusually active around the time of the dinosaur mass extinction, ejecting large amounts of matter into the atmosphere. This choice weakens rather than strengthens the argument. If volcanoes were erupting around the time of the mass extinction and ejecting large amounts of matter into the atmosphere, it could have been the volcanoes, rather than an asteroid impact, that caused the mass extinction.
(B) Fossil evidence indicates that, 65 million years ago, the number of species of dinosaurs inhabiting Earth rapidly decreased. This choice could be tempting because the effect of this choice is that it tends to confirm something stated in the passage, that a mass extinction of dinosaurs occurred 65 million years ago. After all, a rapid decrease in the number of dinosaur species and a mass extinction of dinosaurs are essentially the same thing. So, this choice could appear to make the argument more solid.
However, we don’t need to confirm that a mass extinction occurred, since the passage has already stated as fact that, 65 million years ago, a mass extinction of dinosaurs occurred, and as we have discussed, we can’t strengthen an argument by confirming premises or other facts stated in the passage.
So, actually, this choice has no material effect on what we know about the events described and, thus, has no effect on the strength of the argument.
YOUR CHOICE(C) The chemical composition of the layer of sediment deposited worldwide 65 million years ago resembles that of material found in the area where the asteroid landed in Yucatan. By saying that the chemical composition of the layer of sediment deposited worldwide 65 million years ago resembles that of material found in the area where the asteroid landed in Yucatan, this choice connects the asteroid impact to the sediment layer and to the dust cloud. Thus, it gives us reason to believe that the dust cloud was created by material from the asteroid or material from the Yucatan area that entered the atmosphere when the asteroid landed in Yucatan.
So, this choice provides evidence that supports the explanation given.
Notice that what this answer choice say gives us more reason to believe that the author’s conclusion is accurate.
(D) Near the Yucatan crater, material created by the asteroid impact was found underneath deposits of material that was created thousands of years before the dinosaur mass extinction. This choice could be tempting because it mentions material from the asteroid impact and, thus, seems relevant to the argument.
However, this choice weakens the argument.
By saying that material created by the asteroid impact was found underneath deposits of material created of thousands of years before the dinosaur mass extinction, this choice tends to indicate that the asteroid impacted Earth well before the dinosaur mass extinction, and thus would not have caused the mass extinction.
We have to be careful not to be tempted into choosing a relevant but incorrect choice like this one.
(E) A dust cloud could affect the environment by reflecting much of the sunlight that would otherwise reach the planet. This choice may seem to add information that supports the conclusion, but actually, it only explains how a dust cloud could have severely altered the environment.
The argument has already stated that the layer of sediment indicates that there was a dust cloud that severely altered the environment. Adding information on how such an alteration of the environment could occur does nothing to add support to the conclusion.
Remember, the premises provided in the passage are facts, and explaining some aspect of those facts does not provide additional support for the argument.
Correct Answer: (C) Let’s wrap up our discussion of Strengthen the Argument Questions by discussing how to handle questions in which the stems ask us to find answers that don’t strengthen arguments.
summary:
Lesson Content If the goal in answering a Strengthen question is to find an answer choice that strengthens the argument, then clearly a choice that weakens the argument must be incorrect.
However, when a test-taker is under the pressure of the test, answers that weaken rather than strengthen the arguments in Strengthen questions can be tempting because they are closely related to the given information and, thus, can seem to provide just the type of information needed to answer the question.
title: 4.19 Strengthen Incorrect Choice Type 7: Weakens the Argument

contents:
Lesson Content Strengthen the Argument questions will sometimes include words such as “except,” “not,” or “least” in their question stems to make the questions a bit more challenging.
Each of the following, if true, provides additional support for the above conclusion EXCEPT:
If all of the following are true, which of them does not provide further justification for the contention made?
Assuming that all of the following statements are true, which does the LEAST to strengthen the argument?
Let’s consider the following question stem:
All of the following, if true, strengthen the argument, EXCEPT:
In answering such a question, our job would be to find and eliminate four answer choices that strengthen the argument.
This process seems easy, but test-takers presented with such questions often make a predictable mistake in logic: they search for an answer that weakens the argument.
We must avoid this mistake.
So – just to be clear, if we were faced with a question that asked, “All of the following, if true, strengthen the argument, EXCEPT,” our job would be to eliminate four of the five choices.
💡MUST KNOW:
When Strengthen the Argument questions include words such as “except,” “not,” or “least” in their question stems, our job is to eliminate four choices that do strengthen the argument. The fifth, and correct, answer choice will either weaken the argument or have no effect on the strength of the argument.
Consider the following example:
--- primary_color: green secondary_color: lightgray text_color: black shuffle_questions: false shuffle_answers: false --- ## Example 21 Once native to mainland Australia, but more recently found living only on islands, the Tasmanian Devil thrived in large numbers until about 1990, when its population began to decline. All of the following, if true, would strengthen the biologists’ conclusion, EXCEPT: 1. [ ] (A) The Dingo, which biologists believe to be the primary predator of the Tasmanian Devil on the mainland, has never colonized the islands on which the Devils live. > Correct Answer: (E) 2. [ ] (B) On the islands on which it lives, the natural habitat of the Tasmanian Devil did not significantly decrease during 1990 or the years following 1990. > Correct Answer: (E) 3. [ ] (C) On an island on which no Tasmanian Devils are afflicted with DFTD, the Tasmanian Devil population is flourishing. > Correct Answer: (E) 4. [ ] (D) Over time, Tasmanian Devils have learned to evade traps, snares, and other devices used by hunters. > Correct Answer: (E) 5. [x] (E) Some Tasmanian Devils afflicted with DFTD have been able to reproduce and to effectively care for their young. View Solution Question Level: medium We are told that the Tasmanian Devil thrived in large numbers until about 1990, when its population began to decline. We are also told that, around the same time, Devil Facial Tumor Disease (DFTD), which can lead to starvation and death, was observed in a large number of Tasmanian Devils. As a result of these events’ occurring at about the same time, biologists concluded the following:
Conclusion: DFTD was likely the cause of the decline in the Tasmanian Devil population.
In other words, these biologists concluded that, of all of the things that could cause a decline in the Tasmanian Devil population, the one thing that was responsible was DFTD. This is a big assumption, right? The biologists assumed that there were no other explanations for the decline. Of course, the author is making a cause-and-effect claim.
Let’s think about how we could weaken this argument. One way in which we could weaken the argument would be to come up with some plausible alternative explanations for the decline in the Tasmanian Devil population. In doing so, we’d be effectively attacking the biologists’ assumption that their cause-and-effect claim is correct and that there are no other alternative explanations for the observation.
Let’s be creative and list some potential reasons:
The food source of the Tasmanian Devil disappeared. Hunters began trapping the Tasmanian Devil to sell its fur.
The climate changed in a way that was disadvantageous to the Tasmanian Devil. We could continue adding potential weakeners, but you get the idea. Now, consider the following statement:
The food source of the Tasmanian Devil did not disappear. Would such a statement, if true, strengthen or weaken the argument? Let’s think carefully about this statement. We know that the following statement weakens the argument:
The food source of the Tasmanian Devil disappeared. As we already know, it weakens the argument by introducing an alternative cause. So, if that statement weakens the argument by introducing an alternative cause, then this statement strengthens the argument by eliminating an alternative cause:
The food source of the Tasmanian Devil did not disappear.So, given a cause-and-effect argument, any time we can eliminate an alternative cause for the effect, we strengthen the argument.
Now, since this question is a Strengthen “EXCEPT” question, we have to eliminate four choices that strengthen the argument – those choices are the incorrect choices – and we have to find the one choice that does not strengthen the argument, which is the correct answer.
(A) The Dingo, which biologists believe to be the primary predator of the Tasmanian Devil on the mainland, has never colonized the islands on which the Devils live. By saying that dingoes have not colonized the islands on which the Tasmanian Devils live, this choice eliminates an alternate cause of the decline and, thus, strengthens the argument.
Strengthens? Yes.
(B) On the islands on which it lives, the natural habitat of the Tasmanian Devil did not significantly decrease during 1990 or the years following 1990. By saying that the habitat of the Tasmanian Devils on the islands did not decrease, this choice eliminates an alternative cause of the decline and, thus, strengthens the argument.
Strengthens? Yes.
(C) On an island on which no Tasmanian Devils are afflicted with DFTD, the Tasmanian Devil population is flourishing. By discussing what has occurred on an island where no Tasmanian Devils are afflicted with DFTD, this choice shows the results of eliminating a variable, DFTD. On that island, the Devil population is flourishing.
Since, without DFTD, a population of Tasmanian Devils is flourishing rather than declining, what this choice says tends to confirm the conclusion that DFTD is the cause of the population decline.
Strengthens? Yes.
(D) Over time, Tasmanian Devils have learned to evade traps, snares, and other devices used by hunters. By indicating that it is likely that Tasmanian Devils are not being caught in traps and snares, this choice eliminates an alternate cause of the population decline, traps and snares, and, thus, strengthens the argument.
Strengthens? Yes.
YOUR CHOICE(E) Some Tasmanian Devils afflicted with DFTD have been able to reproduce and to effectively care for their young. By indicating that some Tasmanian Devils afflicted with DFTD have been able to continue to reproduce and care for young, this choice slightly weakens the argument that DFTD is the cause of the population decline.
Strengthens? No.
Correct Answer: (E)
summary:
Lesson Content Strengthen the Argument questions will sometimes include words such as “except,” “not,” or “least” in their question stems to make the questions a bit more challenging.
Each of the following, if true, provides additional support for the above conclusion EXCEPT:
If all of the following are true, which of them does not provide further justification for the contention made?
Assuming that all of the following statements are true, which does the LEAST to strengthen the argument?
title: 4.20 Other Tricky Question Stems: “EXCEPT,” “NOT,” “LEAST”

contents:
Lesson Content A Resolve the Paradox question is based on a passage that presents an apparent paradox, which, in GMAT CR, is a situation in which there exist two facts that seemingly contradict each other or that seemingly cannot both be true at the same time.
In other words, a paradox is composed of two seemingly incompatible statements of fact; the facts, at least without more information, don’t seem to make sense together.
💡MUST KNOW:
In GMAT CR, a paradox is a situation in which there exist two facts that seemingly contradict each other or that seemingly cannot both be true at the same time.
However, in these questions there is no actual paradox, and the correct answer to a Resolve the Paradox question will present more information that reconciles the two seemingly incompatible statements.
💡MUST KNOW:
The correct answer to a Resolve the Paradox question will present additional information that reconciles the two seemingly incompatible statements, showing how they can exist together harmoniously.
Thus, in answering Paradox questions, our job is to find an answer choice that reconciles the apparently contradictory statements, facts, or ideas presented in the stimulus.
💡MUST KNOW:
In answering a Resolve the Paradox question, our job is to choose the answer choice that best explains how both facts presented in the passage could simultaneously be true.
For instance, consider the following two facts, which create a paradox:
Fact 1: A country’s natural resources can be used to generate wealth.
Fact 2: It tends to be the case that countries with abundances of natural resources tend to have less economic growth than countries with fewer natural resources have.
Since the fact that natural resources can be used to generate wealth and the fact that countries with more resources tend to have less economic growth than countries with fewer resources have seem to contradict each other, the situation described by those two statements is paradoxical; it appears that the two statements cannot or would not be true simultaneously.
How could we resolve that paradox? One way is the following:
Countries with abundances of natural resources are significantly more likely to be engaged in internal or external conflicts, which stifle economic growth, than are countries with fewer natural resources.
Notice that the above statement presents us with new information that allows us to see how the seemingly contradictory statements can in fact harmoniously coexist.
Here’s another example of a paradox.
Fact 1: Patients with insomnia, which is the inability to fall asleep or stay asleep, risk falling ill because of the harmful effects caused by lack of sleep.
Fact 2: Most doctors who treat insomnia patients do not instruct their patients to sleep more.
Notice the paradox presented to us.
One way to resolve this paradox is the following:
It is well known that telling insomnia patients that they must sleep more causes them to experience increased worry about not sleeping, leading to even less sleep.
Notice that the above statement presents us with new information that allows us to see how the seemingly contradictory statements can in fact harmoniously coexist.
Now that we understand the basics of how Paradox questions work, let’s consider an example.
--- primary_color: green secondary_color: lightgray text_color: black shuffle_questions: false shuffle_answers: false --- ## Example 1 Critics of the current mayor of East Beach say that, while he has been mayor, little has changed in East Beach. Which of the following statements, if true, would best resolve the apparent contradiction described above? 1. [ ] (A) In East Beach, mayoral elections are not held in the same years in which national elections are held. > Correct Answer: (E) 2. [ ] (B) The most effective leaders of organizations are often unpopular among the organization’s members. > Correct Answer: (E) 3. [ ] (C) Not all residents of East Beach bother to vote in mayoral elections. > Correct Answer: (E) 4. [ ] (D) The current mayor’s first and second terms were not consecutive but, rather, were separated by a term during which someone else was mayor. > Correct Answer: (E) 5. [x] (E) Most residents of East Beach are happy with the city the way it is. View Solution Question Level: easy What is the paradox here?
On one hand, critics of the current mayor of East Beach say that nothing has changed while he has been mayor. On the other hand, he keeps getting reelected.
How could both of these facts be true? The correct answer will provide a piece of information that explains how these two facts coexist.
(A) In East Beach, mayoral elections are not held in the same years in which national elections are held. While this choice could be spun into a story about how people in East Beach don’t pay much attention to mayoral elections because they are not held when national elections are held, that story is not really supported by the given information.
Also, even if that story were true, that story would not explain why this person, and not some other person, has been elected. So, this choice does not resolve the paradox.
(B) The most effective leaders of organizations are often unpopular among the organization’s members. This choice is tempting because it could be perceived as suggesting that the reason for the reelection of this person is that this person is effective. Notice, however, that this choice simply presents us with a generality about leaders, that the most effective leaders of organizations are often unpopular.
On the other hand, the passage does not provide reason to believe that the mayor is either effective or unpopular. Rather, in saying that little has changed, the critics of the current mayor of East Beach seem to be suggesting that he has not been effective.
So, what this choice says is not clearly connected to the scenario presented and, thus, doesn’t resolve the paradox.
(C) Not all residents of East Beach bother to vote in mayoral elections. Notice that, regardless of what this choice says, we still don’t have an explanation for why whoever does vote votes for the current mayor, even though nothing has changed while he has been mayor.
YOUR CHOICE(D) The current mayor’s first and second terms were not consecutive but, rather, were separated by a term during which someone else was mayor. This option could be tempting, because what it says could be perceived as somehow contradicting what the passage says or changing the scenario, since, while the passage says that this person has just been reelected for a third term, this choice indicates that this person’s time as mayor has been broken up by a term during which someone else was mayor.
However, the truth is that what this choice says doesn’t really change anything. Regardless of whether this person’s terms as mayor have been consecutive, this person has still been elected mayor for a third time, and the point made by the critics, that little has changed while this person has been mayor, is unaffected by what this choice says.
CORRECT ANSWER(E) Most residents of East Beach are happy with the city the way it is. The paradox stands on the assumption that what the critics say, that little has changed in East Beach while this person has been mayor, constitutes a reason why this person would not be reelected.
What this choice says attacks that assumption, because, if most residents of East Beach are happy with the city the way it is, the fact that little has changed while this person has been mayor constitutes a reason to reelect this person; he’ll do exactly what most people want – change little. So, this choice resolves the apparent contradiction.
Correct Answer: (E) Notice how answer choice (E) in the mayor of East Beach example resolves the paradox and allows us to say, “Ah – that’s why the mayor keeps getting reelected even though critics say that little has changed.”
As the chapter proceeds, we’ll work through many more examples.
Next, let’s cover some of the common ways in which Resolve the Paradox questions are presented on the GMAT.
summary:
Lesson Content A Resolve the Paradox question is based on a passage that presents an apparent paradox, which, in GMAT CR, is a situation in which there exist two facts that seemingly contradict each other or that seemingly cannot both be true at the same time.
In other words, a paradox is composed of two seemingly incompatible statements of fact; the facts, at least without more information, don’t seem to make sense together.
title: 5.1 Introduction to Resolve the Paradox Questions

contents:
Lesson Content The faster we can recognize what a Critical Reasoning question is asking us to find, the faster we can answer it.
Here are some of the most common question stems you’ll see:
Which of the following, if true, most helps to resolve the apparent discrepancy in the passage above?
Which of the following, if true, would explain the discrepancy described above?
Which one of the following, if true, does most to justify this apparently paradoxical belief?
Which of the following, if true, best reconciles the seeming discrepancy described above?
Which one of the following, if true, does the most to resolve the apparent paradox?
Which of the following, if true, most helps to resolve the apparent paradox?
Which of the following hypotheses best explains the contrast described above?
Which of the following, if true, most helps to account for …?
Which of the following would, if true, best serve as an explanation of the results of …?
Which of the following, if true, most helps to explain the apparent inconsistency?
As we can observe, answering Paradox questions involves explaining how seemingly contradictory facts can harmoniously coexist.
💡MUST KNOW:
Learn to recognize the common question stems associated with Resolve the Paradox questions.
Let’s now consider another example.
--- primary_color: green secondary_color: lightgray text_color: black shuffle_questions: false shuffle_answers: false --- ## Example 2 To help curtail air pollution caused by emissions from gasoline engines, five years ago, the country of Rantala began providing citizens with large tax breaks on the purchase of any zero-emission electric vehicle, with the result that the number of such electric vehicles on Rantalan roadways has increased, while the number of gasoline-powered vehicles on the roadways has decreased. Which one of the following, if true, does the most to resolve the apparent paradox? 1. [ ] (A) The country of Rantala has always used exemplary methods to measure air pollution caused by emissions from gasoline powered vehicles. > Correct Answer: (D) 2. [ ] (B) When countries neighboring Rantala provided tax breaks on the purchase of zero-emission electric vehicles, those countries experienced significant declines in air pollution caused by emissions from gasoline engines. > Correct Answer: (D) 3. [ ] (C) Many of the citizens of Rantala have always chosen to use emission-free forms of transportation, including walking, riding bicycles, and using the electric subway system. > Correct Answer: (D) 4. [x] (D) In Rantala, five years ago, great numbers of massive gasoline-powered machines began working around the clock to extract from the ground metals necessary for manufacturing batteries of electric vehicles. 5. [ ] (E) In addition to providing citizens with large tax breaks on the purchase of any zero-emission electric vehicle, Rantala also imposed clean air taxes on any gasoline-powered vehicle owner. > Correct Answer: (D) View Solution Question Level: easy What is the paradox here?
The passage says that the number of zero-emission electric vehicles on Rantalan roadways has increased, while the number of gasoline-powered vehicles on the roadways has decreased.
At the same time, the passage says also that the amount of air pollution caused by emissions from gasoline engines has increased in Rantala.
How could both of these facts be true? The correct answer will explain how it could be that, even though the first fact is true, the second fact is also true.
(A) The country of Rantala has always used exemplary methods to measure air pollution caused by emissions from gasoline powered vehicles. Since this is a Paradox question, not a Strengthen question, we don’t have to provide a reason to believe anything stated by the passage. Our job is to take the statements as true and explain how the facts presented could be true at the same time.
So, while what this choice says about Rantala using exemplary methods to measure pollution could seem to provide some confirmation that what the passage says about pollution increasing is true, since we don’t need any such confirmation, this choice is not the correct answer.
(B) When countries neighboring Rantala provided tax breaks on the purchase of zero-emission electric vehicles, those countries experienced significant declines in air pollution caused by emissions from gasoline engines. This choice does not explain how the two facts could be true at the same time. In fact, what this choice says makes us even more confused.
After all, if other countries experienced declines in air pollution when they provided tax breaks on the purchase of zero-emission electric vehicles, why did Rantala experience an increase in pollution?
(C) Many of the citizens of Rantala have always chosen to use emission-free forms of transportation, including walking, riding bicycles, and using the electric subway system. This choice does not affect the paradox at all.
OK, so, given what this choice says, many citizens of Rantala were already getting around in emission-free ways even before Rantala began providing tax breaks on the purchase of zero-emission electric vehicles.
All the same, according to the passage, since Rantala began providing those tax breaks, the number of zero-emission electric vehicles on Rantalan roadways has increased, while the number of gasoline-powered vehicles on the roadways has decreased.
So, why then has the amount of air pollution caused by emissions from gasoline engines increased in Rantala?
This choice does not answer that question.
YOUR CHOICE(D) In Rantala, five years ago, great numbers of massive gasoline-powered machines began working around the clock to extract from the ground metals necessary for manufacturing batteries of electric vehicles. This choice explains why the amount of air pollution caused by emissions from gasoline engines has increased in Rantala.
While it’s true that, on the roadways, the number of electric vehicles has increased, and the number of gasoline-powered vehicles has decreased, another change has occurred as well. In order for that increase in the number of electric vehicles to occur, the manufacture of batteries has been necessary, and in order for those batteries to be manufactured, great numbers of massive gasoline-powered machines have been working around the clock to extract metals from the ground.
So, while there are fewer gasoline-powered vehicles on the roads, there are also more gasoline-powered machines working, and the operation of those machines is resulting in the increase in pollution.
So, this choice gives us that “Aha!” moment, resolving the paradox.
(E) In addition to providing citizens with large tax breaks on the purchase of any zero-emission electric vehicle, Rantala also imposed clean air taxes on any gasoline-powered vehicle owner. This choice explains the wrong thing.
This choice could help to explain why the number of gasoline-powered vehicles on Rantala’s roadways has decreased: people reduced their use of gasoline-powered vehicles to avoid paying the clear air tax.
Of course, we don’t have to explain why the number of gasoline-powered vehicles on Rantala’s roadways has decreased; we have to explain why pollution increased, and since there’s no reason to believe that a clean air tax imposed on gasoline-powered vehicle owners would cause an increase in pollution, this choice does not explain the increase in pollution and is not the correct answer.
Correct Answer: (D) Let’s now take a look at some other clues that we can use for identifying and correctly answering Resolve the Paradox questions.
summary:
Lesson Content The faster we can recognize what a Critical Reasoning question is asking us to find, the faster we can answer it.
Here are some of the most common question stems you’ll see:
Which of the following, if true, most helps to resolve the apparent discrepancy in the passage above?
Which of the following, if true, would explain the discrepancy described above?
Which one of the following, if true, does most to justify this apparently paradoxical belief?
title: 5.2 Common Resolve the Paradox Question Stems

contents:
Lesson Content In Resolve the Paradox Questions, the two seemingly contradictory statements will generally be connected by a keyword that indicates a contrast.
yet, but, paradoxically, however, despite, unlike, even though, though, although, instead, rather, surprisingly, unexpectedly
💡MUST KNOW:
Learn to recognize the keywords that indicate a contrast between the two seemingly contradictory facts.
In Paradox questions, contrast keywords often appear between the two paradoxical facts.
Notice the use of the contrast word in the example below.
--- primary_color: green secondary_color: lightgray text_color: black shuffle_questions: false shuffle_answers: false --- ## Example 3 There are in Argentium three bridges across the river that flows through the city, a main bridge and two smaller bridges. Which of the following, if true, best explains the surprising development? 1. [ ] (A) There are bridges just outside of Argentium city limits that motorists traveling to destinations within Argentium can use to cross the river without necessarily making long detours. > Correct Answer: (D) 2. [ ] (B) The two smaller bridges located in Argentium were designed to handle much more vehicular traffic than usually passes over them, even during rush hour. > Correct Answer: (D) 3. [ ] (C) Many residents of Argentium ride bicycles, using special bike lanes, and thus do not contribute to vehicular traffic congestion. > Correct Answer: (D) 4. [x] (D) When aware in advance of issues that may cause traffic congestion, many people in Argentium choose to use public transportation rather than private automobiles. 5. [ ] (E) During the time period when the roadway on the main bridge was closed for maintenance, the pedestrian walkway on the main bridge remained open. > Correct Answer: (D) View Solution Question Level: easy We are presented with an apparent paradox outlined by two statements connected by the contrast word “however.” The facts presented are the following:
Fact 1: Normally, when a traffic incident causes a closure of the main bridge during rush hour, the two smaller bridges become congested with vehicular traffic.
Fact 2: When the roadway on the main bridge was closed for two months for maintenance, the amount of vehicular traffic on the two smaller bridges during rush hour was no greater than usual.
What has to be explained: So, picture it: when a traffic incident causes the main bridge to be closed during rush hour, the two smaller bridges become congested with vehicular traffic. However, when the roadway on the main bridge was closed for two months for maintenance, the amount of vehicular traffic on the two smaller bridges during rush hour was no greater than usual. That’s definitely a paradox, right? What could explain this surprising phenomenon?
(A) There are bridges just outside of Argentium city limits that motorists traveling to destinations within Argentium can use to cross the river without necessarily making long detours. The fact that there are bridges just outside of Argentium city limits that motorists traveling to destinations within Argentium can use to cross the river without necessarily making long detours does not help to resolve the paradox, because these bridges were always there. These other bridges were there when the rush hour crashes occurred, and these other bridges were there during the planned maintenance.
Now, the story would have been different if these new bridges had been constructed just prior to the planned maintenance period. Of course, in that situation, the presence of additional bridges would have helped to explain the surprising development, but, because the bridges were always there, the fact of their presence doesn’t help to resolve the paradox.
(B) The two smaller bridges located in Argentium were designed to handle much more vehicular traffic than usually passes over them, even during rush hour. The fact that the two smaller bridges located in Argentium were designed to handle much more vehicular traffic than usually passes over them, even during rush hour, does not help us to resolve the paradox. Regardless of how much vehicular traffic these two smaller bridges were designed to handle, we still wonder why, when the roadway on the main bridge was closed for two months for maintenance, the amount of vehicular traffic on the two smaller bridges during rush hour did not become greater than usual, given the fact that, when a traffic incident causes a closure of the main bridge during rush hour, the two smaller bridges become congested with vehicular traffic.
(C) Many residents of Argentium ride bicycles, using special bike lanes, and thus do not contribute to vehicular traffic congestion. Regardless of how many residents of Argentium ride bicycles, using special bike lanes, and thus do not contribute to vehicular traffic congestion, the paradox, which involves vehicular traffic congestion, remains unresolved, because the use of bicycles is a constant that existed when the main bridged was closed because of a traffic incident and when the construction was ongoing. So, the fact that residents of Argentium ride bicycles does not explain the difference between the two situations.
YOUR CHOICE(D) When aware in advance of issues that may cause traffic congestion, many people in Argentium choose to use public transportation rather than private automobiles. Let’s think about this. Drivers generally cannot foresee, or plan for, traffic incidents. So, during rush hour, people head to the roads, operating under the assumption that they will get to where they are going without delay. So, when traffic incidents occur, the bridges become backed up. However, when the roadway on the main bridge was closed for two months for maintenance, people could have been, and likely were, aware of the closure in advance of traveling. If they were, they would have used public transportation, with the result that, even though the main bridge was closed, the two smaller bridges did not become congested. So, this choice explains why two different outcomes result from two different types of closures of the main bridge.
(E) During the time period when the roadway on the main bridge was closed for maintenance, the pedestrian walkway on the main bridge remained open. The fact that, during the time period when the roadway on the main bridge was closed for maintenance, the pedestrian walkway on the main bridge remained open does not resolve the paradox. Regardless of whether the pedestrian walkway was open, if the roadway was closed, presumably the bridge was closed to vehicular traffic, a state of affairs that normally results in traffic congestion on the two smaller bridges.
Correct Answer: (D) So far, we’ve gotten to know Resolve the Paradox questions.
summary:
Lesson Content In Resolve the Paradox Questions, the two seemingly contradictory statements will generally be connected by a keyword that indicates a contrast.
yet, but, paradoxically, however, despite, unlike, even though, though, although, instead, rather, surprisingly, unexpectedly
💡MUST KNOW:
Learn to recognize the keywords that indicate a contrast between the two seemingly contradictory facts.
In Paradox questions, contrast keywords often appear between the two paradoxical facts.
Notice the use of the contrast word in the example below.
title: 5.3 Important Resolve the Paradox Keywords

contents:
Lesson Content To answer Resolve the Paradox questions correctly, follow this 5-step strategy.
Step 1 – Carefully read and completely understand the passage.
As is the case in answering any other type of Critical Reasoning question, the foundation of choosing the right answer to a Paradox question is understanding the passage.
Step 2 – Read the question stem and identify what it is asking you to do.
The nature of the information presented in the passage, along with the question stem, should make it easy to tell that you’re faced with a Paradox question.
Step 3 – Bullet point the paradoxical facts.
There might be more than two pieces of information in the passage, but there will be only two contradictory facts that need to be reconciled.
Step 4 – Rephrase the paradox as a question.
Rephrase the paradox as a question:
How can it be the case that Fact 1, even though Fact 2?
For example:
How can it be the case that, on average, lifestyles did not improve, even though the percentage of people with college educations increased? In rephrasing the paradox as a question, what you are doing is making clear what you are seeking to explain.
In answering Critical Reasoning questions of some types, identifying the conclusions is key.
Step 5 – Analyze the answer choices.
For each choice, ask yourself: does this choice explain how it can be the case that Fact 1 is true even though Fact 2 is true? For only one choice will the answer to that question be “Yes.” Remember, if a choice explains anything other than how those facts can be true at the same time, it will not be the correct answer.
Summary of the Strategy for Resolve the Paradox Questions
To summarize, here is the 5-step strategy that you can use to answer Resolve the Paradox questions correctly:
💡MUST KNOW:
Memorize and practice the 5-step strategy for attacking Resolve the Paradox questions.
Let’s apply our strategy to answering an example question.
--- primary_color: green secondary_color: lightgray text_color: black shuffle_questions: false shuffle_answers: false --- ## Example 4 Many of a company’s employees were using the company health plan to pay for services of holistic health practitioners. Which of the following, if true, would best explain why the policy produced the reverse of its expected effect? 1. [ ] (A) Many holistic health practitioners are just as good as licensed physicians at identifying the reasons why a person feels ill. > Correct Answer: (C) 2. [ ] (B) The company’s employees who use the services of holistic health practitioners believe that the methods used by holistic health practitioners are effective in solving health problems. > Correct Answer: (C) 3. [x] (C) The process of determining whether to formally diagnose a person with an illness often costs many times what a visit to a holistic health practitioner costs. 4. [ ] (D) The company could reduce its health plan-related costs by ceasing to require employees to get formal diagnoses from licensed physicians before using the services of holistic health practitioners. > Correct Answer: (C) 5. [ ] (E) In many cases licensed physicians charge about what holistic health practitioners charge for similar services. > Correct Answer: (C) View Solution Question Level: easy Step 1 – Carefully read and completely understand the passage.
We see that, in the scenario presented, a company began requiring its employees to get formal diagnoses from licensed physicians before using the company health plan to pay for the services of holistic health practitioners, and that, after the company instituted this policy, its health plan-related costs unexpectedly increased.
Step 2 – Read the question stem and identify what it is asking you to do.
In reading the passage, we saw that it outlined a paradoxical situation by using two statements connected by the contrast word “however.”
We next see that the question stem is asking us to find the choice that explains why the policy produced the reverse of its expected effect.
Thus, we can tell that we are dealing with a Paradox question and that the correct answer will resolve the apparent paradox or explain what’s going on in the scenario.
3. Bullet point the paradoxical facts.
Fact 1: To ensure that employees would not use the services of holistic health practitioners unnecessarily, a company began requiring its employees to get formal diagnoses from licensed physicians before using the health plan to pay for such services.
Fact 2: After the company instituted this policy, its health plan-related costs increased rather than decreased.
Step 4 – Rephrase the paradox as a question.
What has to be explained: Why would it be that, after a company began requiring its employees to get formal diagnoses from licensed physicians before using the company health plan to pay for the services of holistic health practitioners, the company’s health plan-related costs increased?
Step 5 – Analyze the answer choices.
(A) Many holistic health practitioners are just as good as licensed physicians at identifying the reasons why a person feels ill. This choice merely presents information related to the situation that has no effect on the paradox. The fact that holistic health practitioners are just as good as licensed physicians at identifying the reasons why a person feels ill does not tell us anything about why the company’s costs increased.
(B) The company’s employees who use the services of holistic health practitioners believe that the methods used by holistic health practitioners are effective in solving health problems. This choice explains the wrong thing.
The fact that employees believe that the methods used by holistic health practitioners are effective explains why the employees use the services of holistic health practitioners.
Of course, that’s not what the correct answer has to explain, and this choice does not explain why the company’s costs increased. So, it’s not the correct answer.
YOUR CHOICE(C) The process of determining whether to formally diagnose a person with an illness often costs many times what a visit to a holistic health practitioner costs. This choice explains exactly what we have to explain.
After all, if the process of determining whether to formally diagnose a person with an illness often costs many times what a visit to a holistic health practitioner costs, then by requiring employees to get formal diagnoses from licensed physicians, the company is likely requiring employees to spend more than they would have spent had they simply used the services of holistic health practitioners.
So, this choice explains why the company’s health plan-related costs increased and resolves the apparent paradox.
(D) The company could reduce its health plan-related costs by ceasing to require employees to get formal diagnoses from licensed physicians before using the services of holistic health practitioners. This choice does not have the effect we need at all.
Notice that the relationship between this choice and the statements in the passage is not that this choice explains the statements in the passage. Rather, this choice states a conclusion that follows from the statements in the passage.
After all, what this choice says certainly follows from the fact that, after the company began requiring its employees to get formal diagnoses from licensed physicians before using the health plan to pay for the services of holistic health practitioners, it’s health plan-related costs increased.
(E) In many cases licensed physicians charge about what holistic health practitioners charge for similar services. Notice that, what we have to explain is a difference, in terms of the company’s costs, between employees’ simply using the services of holistic health practitioners and employees’ using the services of physicians before they use the services of holistic health practitioners.
Meanwhile this choice tells us how physicians and holistic health practitioners are similar: what they charge for similar services is, in many cases, about the same.
The fact that physicians and holistic health practitioners are similar in this way does not explain a difference in the company’s costs.
Correct Answer: (C) Let’s now discuss an important, make-or-break component of answering a Resolve the Paradox question.
summary:
Lesson Content To answer Resolve the Paradox questions correctly, follow this 5-step strategy.
Step 1 – Carefully read and completely understand the passage.
As is the case in answering any other type of Critical Reasoning question, the foundation of choosing the right answer to a Paradox question is understanding the passage.
Step 2 – Read the question stem and identify what it is asking you to do.
The nature of the information presented in the passage, along with the question stem, should make it easy to tell that you’re faced with a Paradox question.
title: 5.4 Strategy for Answering Resolve the Paradox Questions

contents:
Lesson Content Precision in reading is one of the most important Critical Reasoning skills to master.
💡MUST KNOW:
In Resolve the Paradox questions, every word matters.
Accordingly, when test-takers get Paradox questions incorrect, they often do so because, when reading the passage of a Paradox question, they do not develop a precise understanding of what the paradox is that they need to resolve.
In answering a Paradox question, we must have a precise understanding of exactly what the paradox is that we must resolve.
💡MUST KNOW:
In answering a Paradox question, we must have a precise understanding of exactly what the paradox is that we must resolve. Without such an understanding, we’ll have little chance of correctly answering the question.
Now, you may be thinking that it’s obvious that, to correctly answer a Paradox question, we have to understand exactly what the paradox is that we must resolve, but here’s why we’re focusing on this component of answering a Paradox question.
To see why giving this component our full attention is so important, let’s consider an example:
For years, the town of Pondville had been overrun with ducks. So, the government of Pondville decided to offer a reward to any citizen who showed proof of having taken a duck to a neighboring town that lacked ducks and released it in the neighboring town. However, two years after the government began offering the reward, Pondville was even more overrun with ducks than it was before the reward was offered.
Which of the following best explains the surprising outcome described above?
If we read the passage carefully, we see that what we have to explain is the following:
Why, after the government of Pondville began offering a reward to any citizen who showed proof of having taken a duck to a neighboring town, did Pondville become even more overrun with ducks?
Here’s the issue though.
For instance, in the case of this question, we might decide that we have to explain the following, which is a vague, inexact version of what we have to explain:
Why didn’t the plan work?
Or, we may decide that we have to explain the following:
Why didn’t the number of ducks in Pondville decrease?
